title: Pipe section for multilateral well construction
abstract: The invention relates to a multilateral pipe section for use in a multilateral well construction. The multilateral pipe section comprising a substantially cylindrically shaped first part (7) having a lining (14) surrounding a port (13) in the first part, the lining (14) being arranged flush with the circumference of the first part (7), a second part (8) adapted to mate with the first part (7) and connect with the first part (7) through the lining (14) of the first part (7) to form a pressure seal connection between the first part (7) and second part (8), the first part (7) has a larger diameter than the second part (8), the second part (8) is adapted to be mated through the port (13) from the inside of the first part (7) towards a lateral borehole after the first part (7) has been installed in the well. The invention further relates to a method for installing a pipe string into a multilateral well construction by using the multilateral pipe section.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A multilateral pipe section for use in a multilateral well construction, characterized in that the multilateral pipe section comprising -a substantially cylindrically shaped first part (7) having a lining (14) surrounding a port (13) in the first part, the lining (14) being arranged flush with the circumference of the first part (7), - a second part (8) adapted to mate with the first part (7) and connect with the first part (7) through the lining (14) of the first part (7) to form a pressure seal connection between the first part (7) and the second part (8), the first part (7) has a larger diameter than the second part (8), the second part (8) is adapted to be mated through the port (13) from the inside of the first part (7) towards a lateral borehole after the first part (7) has been installed in the well. 2. The multilateral pipe section according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the lining (14) and at least the part of the external surface of the second part (8) adapted to connect with the first part (7), is made of a material that has low friction and is adapted to form a seal between the connection between the first part (7) and second part (8). 3. The multilateral pipe section according to claim 1, wherein at least a part of the multilateral pipe section is made from a composite material, such as thermoplastic. 4. The multilateral pipe section according to claim 1, 2 or 3, wherein at least the second part (8) are made of a material that has some degree of flexibility, the parts (7, 8) may be forced into connection by the principles of snap fit, press fit, interference fit. 5. The multilateral pipe section according to claim 1, 2, 3 or 4, wherein the lining (14) has an inclined surface from the inside surface (14a) of the first part to the outside surface (14b) of the first part. 6. The multilateral pipe section according to any of the claims 1-5, wherein the first part (7) has an elliptical shaped port (13) built into the side wall of the first part (7). 7. The multilateral pipe section according to claim 6, wherein the elliptical port (13) is formed so that the second part (8) is adapted to exit through the elliptical port (13) from the inside of the first part (7) and form a pressure 

 seal around the port (13). 8. The multilateral pipe section according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the multilateral pipe section (6) is equipped with a resistivity imaging tool (12) on the outer surface of the first part (7), the resistivity imaging tool (12) is adapted to position the first part (7) in alignment with a lateral borehole (21, 22). 9. The multilateral pipe section according claim 8, wherein the resistivity imaging tool (12) is configured to use electromagnetic wave reflection processing. 10. A method for installing a pipe string into a multilateral well construction, comprising a multilateral pipe section according to any of the claim 1-9, wherein the method comprises the steps of a) installing a pipe string including the first part of the multilateral pipe section (7) into a drilled main bore (20), b) positioning the port (13) of the first part (7) in alignment with a drilled lateral bore (21, 22) of the drilled main bore (20), c) guiding the second part of the multilateral pipe section (8) through a port (13) from inside the first part (7) that is positioned in the main bore (20) towards the lateral borehole (21, 22), d) locking the first part (7) and the second part (8) together in a sealable connection. 11. The method according to claim 9, wherein the method comprising a repetition of the steps a-d) for a second lateral borehole (22).
